title: Online latent degradation filter as features
summary: Recent F1 tire modeling work treats degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets, fuel adjustment, and uncertainty, which is closer to race strategy state than raw cumulative degradation.
rationale: Recent F1 tire modeling work treats degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets, fuel adjustment, and uncertainty, which is closer to race strategy state than raw cumulative degradation. Your current features use fixed thresholds; an online filtered pace/degradation state could capture within-stint drift and warm-up more cleanly.
implementation_hint: For each Race-Year-Driver sequence, build leakage-safe recursive features only from prior laps: expected fuel-adjusted lap time, EWMA/1D Kalman-style latent pace, residual-to-latent pace, residual volatility, post-pit warm-up age, and uncertainty bands. Reset state on PitStop=1. Feed these as extra numeric features into the existing tabular model.
expected_effect: Should improve ranking around borderline laps where raw TyreLife and Cumulative_Degradation are ambiguous but latent pace deterioration is already visible.
risk: If the filter is poorly specified, it can just repackage noisy lap-time deltas. Must exclude pit-in/out artifacts and keep updates strictly past-only.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
